what_it_does: This file is a ledger entry that records an attachment in the design-assets channel, linking an attachment id to an image asset target and an addition timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter with metadata only and no body content
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
id prefix att- for attachment records
concerns: Target name 'image (14)' is generic and gives no descriptive content
No content hash, MIME type, size, or creator metadata
No link to a specific message, thread, or conversation context
Target contains spaces and parentheses rather than a normalized identifier
notes: A minimal attachment ledger record that points an attachment id to an image asset in the design-assets channel.
questions: Which message or thread is this attachment associated with?
Is 'image (14)' a stable, unique asset identifier or an auto-generated display name?
What is the actual content, format, or dimensions of the referenced image?
Why is the added timestamp in 2026?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record an attachment, and the file does exactly that by storing attachment metadata and a target reference.
intention_signals: It appears intended to register an image asset attachment in the design-assets channel so the asset can be tracked and referenced through a persistent attachment id.
novelty: The target uses an image:// channel/asset scheme with a parenthetical ordinal name ('image (14)') instead of a conventional file path or UUID.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel attachment identifier and an image asset target in the design-assets channel.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment ledger entry att-9a5c6d4c records an images-type attachment in the design-assets channel targeting image://design-assets/image (14), added on 2026-06-22T12:25:33.102207+00:00.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to catalog and reference an image asset attached to the design-assets channel.
